methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Corvex Systems is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Jason Blick that has raised $5M+. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 private robotics firms and 1 public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ), though same-stage and adjacent-stage peers within this sector proved insufficient in number for a reliable peer-median valuation.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable here, using total capital raised ($5M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage transitions. This yields an implied valuation of $12M. A peer-median approach could not be reliably applied: while 11 robotics peers exist in the broader dataset, zero companies matched the same-stage and valuation-tier filters required to establish a credible narrow peer set (minimum 3 datapoints). Drawing on all-sector median values would introduce unacceptable estimation error given Corvex's specific frontier-tech positioning.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to a single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $11M (low) to $14M (high), with a median of $12M. Key uncertainties include the absence of same-stage private comparables that would validate or adjust the stage-progression multiplier, and limited public market data to cross-check robotics sector trading multiples. Any future valuation update should prioritize collecting additional Seed-stage robotics transactions to tighten the band around this single-method estimate.
